title: The Titan Rises
tagline: 31 hours. GRANITE answers. And NOVA's contact is older than the city.
villain_mandate: The Titan is the overwhelming threat this episode — RUMBLE is absent but his design is felt in every scene. The Titan must feel genuinely impossible: ancient, massive, indifferent to mech-scale weapons. GRANITE returns early. NOVA's contact changes the team's understanding of what they're fighting.

titan.name: COLOSSUS — The Buried One
titan.description: A Titan sealed under the city 3,000 years ago. Not evil — ancient and disoriented. Forty meters tall. Stone and metal fused over millennia. Does not communicate. Does not reason. Simply moves toward open sky, the thing it has not seen in 3,000 years. Collateral damage is incidental, not intentional. Stopping it means containing it, not destroying it.
titan.note: The Titan being non-evil is important — it changes how the team approaches the problem and what victory looks like

nova_contact.name: ELDER-7
nova_contact.description: An ancient autonomous mech — the oldest operational unit in existence, 2,800 years old, built before the Titan was sealed. He was present at the original sealing. He knows exactly what COLOSSUS is and what the original team used. NOVA found him in an archive designation. He is small, weathered, slow-moving, and knows more about this situation than anyone.
nova_contact.visual: Ancient, small, oxidized bronze, moves carefully. Speaks in complete measured sentences.

scenes.title: The Message
scenes.narration: GRANITE received BLAZE's message at hour three of recovery. His sleeve was at forty percent — not the seventy-two hours NOVA had projected for full restoration. He stood up. He messaged back three words: On my way. NOVA saw the message. She didn't argue. She sent him the Titan's geological profile instead. He read it en route. He arrived at hour six. His sleeve at forty-two percent. He said: 'What do we know?'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. GRANITE reading BLAZE's message, reinforcement sleeve at 40%. Standing up. Sending three words. Then arriving — sleeve not fully restored but steady. His expression: not heroic, just present. What needs doing needs doing. 16:9.

scenes.title: ELDER-7
scenes.narration: NOVA's contact arrived the same hour: ELDER-7, ancient and small, moving carefully. He looked at the team. He said: 'I remember the first sealing. We used four resonance anchors, positioned at cardinal points, tuned to COLOSSUS's specific frequency. We had three weeks to prepare.' BLAZE said: 'We have twenty-five hours.' ELDER-7 said: 'Then you'll need to do it faster than we did.' He sat down. 'I'll help you build the anchors.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. ELDER-7 — ancient, small, weathered bronze — meeting the team. He's 2,800 years old and it shows but he's still precise and present. He explains the original sealing. The team listens. NOVA's display shows the four-anchor configuration from 3,000 years ago. 25-hour countdown. ELDER-7 rolling up his metaphorical sleeves. 16:9.

scenes.title: COLOSSUS Surfaces
scenes.narration: At hour eight, COLOSSUS broke the surface. Not violently — the plaza cracked outward in a circle, stone pushed aside methodically. A hand. Then a shoulder. Then the full form, forty meters, fused stone and ancient metal, standing in the center of Metron City looking at the sky for the first time in three millennia. It didn't attack. It looked up. The city around it damaged from its emergence, but COLOSSUS itself stood still, processing open air.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. COLOSSUS emerging from the city center — enormous, ancient, stone and metal fused over thousands of years. Forty meters tall, standing in the wreckage of the central plaza looking upward at the sky with what might be wonder if Titans could wonder. Not attacking. Just looking up. The team watching from a distance. 16:9.

scenes.title: How Big Is Too Big
scenes.narration: STORM ran a perimeter pass at full speed — one full circuit around COLOSSUS. She reported: weapons ineffective at this scale, conventional approach useless. BLAZE's fire-jets: scale of an insect to it. GRANITE's full power at forty percent sleeve: it would feel a tap. ELDER-7 confirmed: 'You cannot damage it. The original team didn't damage it either. You have to speak its frequency.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. Scale comparison — STORM circling COLOSSUS at full speed, shown as a tiny silver dot against the massive form. BLAZE's fire-jets against it: minuscule. GRANITE looking up at it. ELDER-7 beside them, patient. 'You cannot damage it.' The team reckoning with a genuinely different category of problem. 16:9.

scenes.title: Building the Anchors
scenes.narration: ELDER-7 guided the anchor construction — he had the original schematics in three-thousand-year-old memory. NOVA translated them to current materials. STORM sourced components at speed. BLAZE and GRANITE built. Fifteen hours. The anchors weren't weapons — they were tuning forks. Each one broadcast COLOSSUS's specific dormancy frequency. Four anchors, cardinal positions, signal overlap at the center. COLOSSUS had to be in the center when all four fired.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. CONSTRUCTION MONTAGE — ELDER-7 directing with ancient schematics, NOVA translating to modern designs, STORM returning with components at speed, BLAZE and GRANITE building. The anchors look different from modern tech — tuning-fork shapes, resonance chambers. 15-hour countdown. COLOSSUS moving slowly in the background, still looking at the sky. 16:9.

scenes.title: Getting COLOSSUS to the Center
scenes.narration: The hardest part: COLOSSUS had to be at the exact center position when the anchors fired. It wasn't hostile, but it wasn't responsive either. Three thousand years of underground stasis — it moved toward warmth and light instinctively. NOVA identified the highest light source in Metron City: the broadcast tower on the east hill. If that tower went to maximum output, COLOSSUS would move toward it — directly through the anchor center point. BLAZE said: 'We're using light as a leash.' ELDER-7 said: 'The original team used the sun.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA's positioning plan — light source on east hill, COLOSSUS's likely movement path shown in arc passing through anchor center point. BLAZE: 'we're using light as a leash.' ELDER-7's quiet acknowledgment. The Titan in background moving slowly, drawn toward light. The geometry has to be exact. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Anchors Fire
scenes.narration: The broadcast tower went to full output at hour twenty-three. COLOSSUS moved — slow, inevitable, directly toward it, and directly through the anchor center. BLAZE triggered the sequence. ELDER-7 said: 'Now.' All four anchors fired simultaneously. The dormancy frequency built — not a weapon, a lullaby at the right pitch. COLOSSUS stopped. It looked at the light for four seconds. Then it sat down. Then it lay down. Then it was still.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. CLIMAX — broadcast tower blazing with light, COLOSSUS moving toward it through the four anchor positions. All four anchors fire: resonance waves visible, overlapping, building. COLOSSUS stopping. Looking at the light. Then slowly, massively sitting down. Then lying back. Then still. The city damaged but standing. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Comes Next
scenes.narration: COLOSSUS was dormant but not sealed. ELDER-7 was clear: the original sealing had required three weeks and specialist equipment. They had used emergency dormancy — a temporary measure. COLOSSUS would wake again in approximately forty days. They had forty days to build a permanent solution. NOVA said: 'I'll start now.' ELDER-7 said: 'I'll stay.' GRANITE's sleeve was at forty-four percent. He said nothing about it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. Aftermath — COLOSSUS lying dormant in the damaged city center. The team around ELDER-7 hearing the timeline: 40 days. NOVA already working. ELDER-7 settling in. GRANITE standing, sleeve indicator shown at 44%. He says nothing about it. 40-day countdown begins. 16:9.

scenes.title: RUMBLE Watches
scenes.narration: RUMBLE watched the dormancy field settle from a distance — far enough that no sensor picked him up. He ran the numbers: the team had handled COLOSSUS in twenty-three hours. He had estimated twenty-six. They were three hours faster than his model. He made a note. He looked at ELDER-7 — an unaccounted-for variable. He added: NOVA HAS HISTORICAL RESOURCES. He closed his log. He had what he came to learn.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RUMBLE at distance watching the dormancy field settle, running numbers, updating his log. ELDER-7 noted as unaccounted-for variable. Adding NOVA HAS HISTORICAL RESOURCES. Closing log. He is satisfied — they performed better than expected. That is good information. He leaves. Season finale stakes clear. 16:9.

scenes.title: 40 Days
scenes.narration: NOVA projected the permanent sealing requirements: materials, power, time. ELDER-7 added what he knew from three thousand years ago. The list was long. The forty-day window was tight. But there was something else on NOVA's display — a secondary reading from RUMBLE's array. Eleven points dormant. One point still active. The twelfth point — the central plaza node — was still transmitting. RUMBLE had left it running. NOVA said: 'He's not done.' Episode 11.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA's display showing the sealing requirements and 40-day timeline. Then the secondary reading: eleven array points dormant, but twelfth point still active, still transmitting. RUMBLE left it running deliberately. BLAZE's expression: this isn't over. It was never just about COLOSSUS. 16:9.

youtube_title: THE TITAN RISES! Can the Team Stop COLOSSUS?! 😱 | Mech Legends Episode 10
next_episode_preview: Episode 11 — The Twelfth Point. RUMBLE left one node running. ELDER-7 knows why. The team has 39 days and one active transmission that could wake COLOSSUS before they're ready.
